data.title: MN Holdings says RM38m waste-to-energy project awarded by Idiqa
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): MN Holdings Bhd (KL:MNHLDG) on Thursday revealed that Idiqa Holdings Sdn Bhd awarded it a RM37.91 million contract for the engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) of a waste-to-energy interconnection facility in the central region of Peninsular Malaysia. 

The disclosure came in response to a query from Bursa Malaysia on May 19. The query had required the company to respond within one market day of the letter.

MN Holdings had previously withheld the customer's name in its May 14 filing due to a non-disclosure agreement. 

The project is scheduled to commence on May 14, 2025, with completion targeted for March 31, 2027.  

MN Holdings is principally involved in underground utilities engineering services solutions, and substation engineering services and solutions. It was listed on April 28, 2022. 

At market close, MN Holdings shares were five sen or 3.8% lower at RM1.25 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM703.2 million. 

Read also:
MN Holdings bags RM37.9 mil waste-to-energy facility project from 'Company A'
Frankly Speaking: Who decides what to disclose to investing public?

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Symphony Life plans RM23m private placement to fund Lembah Ledang project
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Symphony Life Bhd (KL:SYMLIFE) has proposed to raise up to RM23.53 million via a private placement of 10% of its enlarged share base to fund a joint development project.

The exercise will comprise up to 107.42 million shares to be issued to independent third parties that would be identified later, according to the property developer’s bourse filing on Thursday.

The issue price will also be fixed later. The estimated RM23.53 million in proceeds to be raised is based on an illustrative issue price of 21.9 sen, a 5.4% discount to Symphony Life shares’ five-day volume-weighted average price of 23.15 sen up to May 13.

The group said RM23.29 million in proceeds will go towards the Lembah Ledang project, a mixed-development project Symphony Life is to jointly develop with Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) and JBiz Development Sdn Bhd, with a gross development value of RM5.3 billion.

The remainder of proceeds will defray the exercise’s expenses. The maximum RM23.53 million in proceeds is assuming placement shares are issued based on Symphony Life’s share base if all treasury shares are resold in the open market and its warrants are fully exercised.

Back in December 2016, Symphony Life formed a joint venture (JV) company with PNB and JBiz Development, called PJS Damansara Sdn Bhd, to acquire and develop a 19.1-acre parcel of land in Bukit Damansara, Kuala Lumpur.

PJS Damansara is 33.3% equally owned by the trio.

The development project comprises two blocks of office towers with a retail podium and eight blocks of service residences with a retail podium.

It is pending a development order from the Kuala Lumpur City Hall, expected to be obtained by the fourth quarter of 2025 (4Q2025).

Construction is expected to commence in 3Q2026. The project’s gross development cost is listed as RM4.4 billion, according to Symphony Life.

Separately, Symphony Life announced the resignation of its executive chairman Chin Jit Pyng, effective immediately.

Chin, 68, stepped down “to pursue personal interest”.

Shares in Symphony Life ended half a sen or 2.27% lower at 21.5 sen on Thursday, valuing the company at RM154.04 million.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Trump’s ‘little problem’ with Tim Cook is a big one for Apple
data.description:
(May 22): Apple Inc has become a frequent target for attacks by President Donald Trump, a factor that has held the stock back as other big tech companies have rebounded over the past month.

The iPhone maker was the biggest company in the world at the start of May, but it has fallen since then to third, behind Microsoft Corp and Nvidia Corp.

It underperformed again Wednesday, falling 2.3% in a broadly negative session after OpenAI announced the acquisition of a start-up co-founded by Apple veteran Jony Ive that is focused on artificial intelligence-fueled hardware. The prospect of shiny new alternatives to Apple’s devices added to concerns about the company’s struggle to compete in the AI arms race.

But a more unusual threat stems from Trump’s focus on the company over its global manufacturing process. Earlier this month, Trump said he “had a little problem with Tim Cook,” and said that he had asked Apple’s chief executive officer to stop building plants in India.

“It’s a red flag for me that Trump continues to single out Apple and seems to have something against them,” said Randy Hare, director of equity research at Huntington National Bank. “It doesn’t mean that Trump is going to do anything more, but you can’t predict what’s going to happen, and that makes me cautious.”

Political risk has been a driver of markets overall this year, as seen in Wednesday’s stock sell-off over concerns about the ballooning deficit, an issue that prompted Moody’s to downgrade the US credit rating. Investors are also monitoring the latest developments regarding the Trump administration’s signature tax bill.

Apple’s fate has been more closely tied to the trade war and it has avoided the worst-case scenario that seemed plausible last month. While shares saw incredible tariff-related volatility and have recently been rangebound, the stock is still up 17% from the low it hit after the tariffs were first announced in early April.

Not long after, the Trump administration exempted key categories of electronics — including smartphones and computers — from its so-called reciprocal tariffs, and the US and China agreed to temporarily lower tariffs on each other’s products.

The CBOE Apple VIX, which tracks a market estimate of future volatility for the stock, has fallen by more than half since hitting a five-year high a month ago.

Trump also praised Nvidia’s CEO for attending an investment forum in Saudi Arabia while noting that Cook didn’t.

“Trump pointing out that Cook isn’t at some summit makes the hair on the back of my neck stand up,” said Huntington National Bank’s Hare. “It doesn’t seem normal to me. Anyone who tells you they can quantify this risk is wrong.”

The stock is down 1.2% over the past month, compared with a rally of 13% for the Nasdaq 100 Index.

Resolving Trump’s concerns around Apple’s foreign manufacturing may be tricky, given the near-impossibility of building iPhones and other hardware in the US, especially in the short term.

Lamar Villere, partner and portfolio manager at Villere & Co said he thinks the concerns about the threats from Trump may be overblown.

“It gets a lot of attention from Trump, not all of it good, but I think investors are growing a callous to his complaints,” Villere said. “It isn’t in any real trouble for not building in the US, and we’re not going to see legislation come out based on anger against one company.”

But the ire targeted at Apple is only one of the geopolitical risks that the company is facing from Trump’s policies. Financial results released earlier this month reinforced concerns about a slowdown in the China market as well as the impact of tariffs.

At least two firms downgraded the stock following the report, cementing its reputation as one of the least-loved big-tech names. It is also “the most under-owned mega cap tech stock,” according to Morgan Stanley, which examined its average weight in the top 100 actively managed institutional portfolios and compared that to its weight in the S&P 500 index exiting the first quarter of 2025.

The political uncertainty comes on top of other headwinds that have kept some investors at bay, including growth concerns and a high multiple. Apple trades at 26.6 times estimated earnings, well above its 10-year average of 21. It also trades at a premium to megacap peers that are expected to grow faster this year, despite its ongoing difficulties in the crucial realm of artificial intelligence.

To Huntington’s Hare, this is a more fundamental issue than the political backdrop.

“We’re not sure how Apple is going to offer AI or when, and that lack of innovation or new products keeps us on the sidelines,” he said. “It’s not growing the way it used to and the multiple continues to be on the high end of things. Those are good reasons to be underweight even before you get to the political climate.”

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Batu Kawan's 2Q net profit slightly up on higher palm product prices
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Batu Kawan Bhd (KL:BKAWAN) posted a 3.7% year-on-year increase in net profit for the second quarter, carried by higher palm product prices, offset by lower sales volume and fair value loss on unharvested oil palm.

The net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (2QFY2025) edged up to RM87.89 million from RM84.72 million a year earlier, according to the plantation group’s bourse filing on Thursday.

Revenue for the quarter rose 15% year-on-year to RM6.51 billion from RM5.66 billion, thanks to the higher crude palm oil (CPO) and palm kernel selling prices realised by the plantation segment.

Batu Kawan declared an interim dividend of 20 sen per share, payable on July 31.

The group said its earnings growth was offset by a drop in CPO and palm kernel sales volumes and a fair value loss of RM53.42 million, versus a RM1.8 million gain in 2QFY2024, on valuation of unharvested fresh fruit bunches.

Batu Kawan's 47.74%-owned Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd (KL:KLK) logged a 31.8% increase in net profit to RM154.27 million from RM117.07 million for 2QFY2024, as revenue rose 16.2% to RM6.34 billion from RM5.46 billion.

For the first half of FY2025, Batu Kawan’s net profit was up 9.7% to RM215.48 million from RM196.46 million in the same period a year ago, as cumulative revenue climbed 9.9% to RM12.63 billion from RM11.49 billion.

Looking ahead, Batu Kawan said CPO prices are expected to soften to RM3,800 to RM4,200 per tonne. “The group’s plantations segment will continue to be the primary earnings driver,” the group said.

“Given the challenging macroeconomic outlook and volatility in commodity markets following the recent tariff developments, the group is cautious for the remainder of FY2025,” it added.

Shares in Batu Kawan were untraded on Thursday. On Wednesday, the counter closed at RM19.06, valuing the group at RM7.62 billion.

Read also:
Kuala Lumpur Kepong's 2Q net profit up 32%, pays 20 sen dividend

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: DRB-Hicom’s 1Q net profit down 81% on lower sales and higher costs in its auto, postal, and property segments
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): DRB-Hicom Bhd (KL:DRBHCOM) first quarter net profit plummeted 80.6% year-on-year (y-o-y) dragged by lower sales and higher costs at its automotive, postal and property segments.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) fell to RM17.72 million from RM91.54 million in the same period a year ago, according to the group’s bourse filing on Thursday.

The lower earnings came as revenue fell 5.1% y-o-y to RM4.11 billion from RM4.33 billion, on lower contributions from its automotive, properties, aerospace and postal segments. 

Sales at its auto division was down 6.1% to RM2.8 billion on lower sales volume of Proton vehicles from its 50.1%-owned Proton Holdings Bhd and lower revenue from manufacturing and engineering companies.

Revenue from properties fell 41% to RM46.6 million, while postal revenue dropped 0.7% to RM457.1 million.

The group’s banking and services segments helped to partly offset the declines in revenue. Banking posted a 4.4% increase in revenue to RM510.41 million on higher financing income, underpinned by an expanding customer base and sustained growth in financing volume.

Revenue for the services segment was up 8% with increased commercial vehicle inspection volumes within the group’s vehicle inspection operations.

No dividend was declared for the quarter under review.

The group said in a separate statement its new Proton model sales in 2025 will depend on market conditions, citing the Malaysian Automotive Association’s forecast of lower vehicle sales due to weaker demand, reduced order backlogs, and possible fuel subsidy changes.

DRB-Hicom said it is focusing on digital transformation in key sectors like banking and postal services to improve efficiency. It is also strengthening business fundamentals in aerospace, defence, services, and properties for long-term sustainability.

The group expects a moderate outlook for the financial year ending December 2025.

Shares in DRB-Hicom ended one sen or 1.21% lower at 81.5 sen, valuing the group at RM1.57 billion.
 

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Barakah to be suspended, faces delisting after Bursa rejects regularisation plan extension
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Barakah Offshore Petroleum Bhd (KL:BARAKAH) will have its shares suspended from trading on May 30 and may be delisted on June 4, after it failed to obtain additional time to submit a regularisation plan.

In a filing with the exchange on Thursday, the Practice Note 17 (PN17) company said Bursa Securities had rejected its application for an extension as "the company has not demonstrated to the satisfaction of Bursa Securities any material development towards the finalisation and submission of the regularisation plan".

If the company files an appeal against the delisting, the June 4 delisting will be deferred until a decision on the appeal is made. However, Bursa Securities will proceed with the suspension of the company’s securities on May 30, even if a decision on the appeal is still pending.

Barakah submitted its latest extension application on April 30. Prior to that, the company had received several extensions from the regulator, with the most recent one allowing it until May 14 to submit its plan.

Notably, Barakah has withdrawn the proposed regularisation plan that was submitted on Oct 30 last year, following the receipt of an adjudication sum of RM78.8 million and updates on its tendering contracts.

The earlier plan had included a proposed capital reduction of RM195 million, a three-into-one share consolidation, and the issuance of 62.5 million new shares to its executive chairman, Datuk Seri Nik Hamdan Daud, to raise RM7.5 million.

Barakah triggered PN17 status in March 2019 after it defaulted on payments to EXIM Bank for a vessel.

The company’s shares closed unchanged at three sen on Thursday, valuing the company at RM30.1 million.

Read also:
Barakah drafts new PN17 regularisation plan after receiving RM79 mil adjudication sum

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Sunway's 1Q net profit rises almost 11% on data centre jobs, higher returns from property investments
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Sunway Bhd’s (KL:SUNWAY) net profit rose nearly 11% to RM190.6 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025, up from RM172.2 million a year ago, driven by faster progress in data centres jobs in its construction segment and property investments.

Revenue jumped 66% to RM2.4 billion, compared to RM1.5 billion last year.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said construction profits surged 170% to RM114.5 million due to faster progress on data centre projects, making it the biggest profit contributor for the group.

Its property investment segment saw a 33% profit increase to RM92.3 million, supported by higher revenue from property assets, better leisure operations and increased contribution from the Sunway real estate investment trust.

The trading and manufacturing segment gained from stronger domestic sales, while its quarry segment saw higher sales volume and selling prices. These gains helped offset weaker results in property development, healthcare and other areas.

Sunway said profit before tax for its healthcare segment dropped 13.6% to RM31.8 million from RM36.8 million a year earlier due to start-up losses at the newly opened Sunway Medical Centre (SMC) Damansara and SMC Ipoh.

However, existing hospitals — SMC Sunway City, Velocity and Penang — performed better, supported by more licensed beds and higher patient numbers. As of May 2025, total licensed beds increased to 1,647 with the opening of SMC Ipoh and bed additions at SMC Sunway City.

Sunway expects continued growth in the construction industry, supported by investments in digital infrastructure. It has secured RM2.2 billion in new orders so far and targets RM4.5-RM6 billion for the year, with a strong RM6.6 billion order book and ongoing data centre projects.

“We are encouraged by the strong start to the year. The group remains steadfast in executing its growth strategies across all business segments, ensuring its growth momentum throughout the year and into the future,” Sunway Group’s president Tan Sri Chew Chee Kin said in a statement.

He said Sunway is strengthening its growth with two major mixed-use transit-oriented developments in Johor and Seremban, which are expected to transform both cities.

Meanwhile, Sunway Healthcare Group has partnered with Putrajaya Holdings to build a 300-bed specialist hospital in Putrajaya, increasing its future bed capacity to over 3,000.

He said the group will cautiously navigate the headwinds surrounding tariffs and policy uncertainties with its performance for the year expected to remain strong.

The stock closed down 1.2% to RM4.83 on Thursday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM30 billion. The stock is up 0.84% this year.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Gold rally lifts Tomei sales, earnings to new record in 1Q
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Tomei Consolidated Bhd (KL:TOMEI) reported a 33% rise in net profit for the first quarter, driven by improved profit margins on the back of a gold price rally towards fresh highs this year.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) rose to RM27.8 million, or 20.09 sen per share, from RM20.9 million or 15.11 sen per share, according to the company’s filing with Bursa Malaysia.

Revenue climbed 8% year-on-year (y-o-y) to RM348 million from RM322.8 million, supported by stronger contributions from the retail segment, thanks to higher retail gold prices.

The gold spot price has been on an upward trajectory since the start of the year, gaining over 74% to reach US$3,288 (RM14,056) per ounce as of 7pm on Thursday.

This marks Tomei’s best-ever quarterly revenue and net profit since its listing on Bursa Malaysia nearly two decades ago in 2006. Despite the strong performance, no dividend was proposed for the quarter.

By segment, Tomei's retail revenue rose 3% y-o-y to RM286.7 million from RM277.6 million, contributing to a 34% increase in profit before tax. Manufacturing and wholesale revenue, however, declined 8% to RM63 million, compared to RM68.6 million a year earlier.

Looking ahead, the group said it will continue to monitor the evolving economic landscape and take the necessary steps to sustain its profitability in the coming quarters.

Shares in Tomei ended one sen or 0.6% higher at RM1.72 on Thursday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM238.4 million.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Cahya Mata Sarawak's 1Q profit down 34% on unfavourable forex, prolonged monsoon season
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Cahya Mata Sarawak Bhd’s (KL:CMSB) net profit fell 33.8% to RM25.34 million in its first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) compared to RM38.25 million a year ago, dragged by unfavourable foreign exchange (forex), and lower contribution from associates and joint ventures.

In the quarter under review, the Sarawak-based conglomerate incurred an unrealised forex loss of RM5.75 million compared to an unrealised forex gain of RM16.31 million in 1QFY2024, it said in its results filing.

Contribution from associates and joint ventures fell 41.5% to RM12.45 million from RM21.27 million a year ago.

Quarterly revenue for 1QFY2025 declined 11.3% to RM246.13 million, down from RM277.37 million in the same period last year, attributed to lower revenue in the oiltools division and cement segment.

In a separate statement, Cahya Mata highlighted the lower revenue was due to the severe and prolonged rainy season in Sarawak, which has significantly disrupted the group’s operations in the first quarter.

In terms of segment performance, the forex loss contributed to its phosphate segment's widened losses, while the weather dragged its oiltools segment as rig activity slowed. 

Meanwhile, its property development business turned to loss in absence of a land sale profit last year, while its investment segment profit also shrank.

Improvement was nonetheless seen in its cement and road maintenance segment earnings.

Going forward, the group expects weather conditions to improve for the remainder of the year, supporting the resumption of construction activities.

It also noted that construction prospects remain strong in Sarawak, while the recent weakening of the US dollar against the ringgit is expected to benefit its supply chain costs.

Shares of Cahya Mata settled two sen or 1.6% lower at RM1.22 on Thursday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.31 billion. The stock has shown strong performance recently, climbing nearly 52% from its 80 sen low in mid-April.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: RCE Capital's 4Q net profit falls 43% on writedown, provisions
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Consumer finance company RCE Capital Bhd (KL:RCECAP) said its net profit fell 43% in its final quarter from a year earlier mainly due to writedowns and provisions for bad debts.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) was RM16.63 million, or 1.13 sen per share, RCE Capital said in an exchange filing. An impairment on goodwill of RM18.99 million and an allowances for impairment loss on receivables of RM15.02 million was booked during the quarter.

Revenue, meanwhile, was up 13% year-on-year at RM92.82 million, largely contributed by higher early settlement profit and fee income. The company declared a second interim dividend of 3.5 sen per share, payable on June 30.

RCE Capital, which provides loans mostly to civil servants with repayments through direct salary deduction, said it expects to be profitable for the coming financial year.

“Prudent credit risk management and quality financing growth remain the focus areas in safeguarding its asset quality amid challenging operating environment,” the company added.

Net profit for the full financial year was down 24% to RM105.54 million while revenue decreased 2.9% to RM331.67 million. Financing receivables slipped 1.3% to RM1.72 billion at the end of March from FY2024.

Shares of RCE Capital rose three sen or 2.3% to RM1.33, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.97 billion ahead of the results announcement. The counter has declined 14% year-to-date.

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title: Dutch Lady’s 1Q net profit down 6%, pays 25 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Dairy company Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd (KL:DLADY) reported a 6.1% decline in net profit to RM25.03 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) from RM26.66 million a year earlier, on lower operating profit.

Operating profit for the quarter stood at RM34.8 million, down 4.1% from RM36.3 million in 1QFY2024. The operating profit includes RM8.3 million in one-off costs largely related to transition to the new distribution centre.

As a result, earnings per share was lower at 39.1 sen for 1QFY2025 compared with 41.7 sen for 1QFY2024.

Quarterly revenue, however, rose 2.9% to RM373.4 million, up from RM362.77 million in 1QFY2024, primarily driven by higher sales in its core range of liquid milk products, infant formula and toddler (IFT) products, strong momentum in its professional range, as well as growing revenue from newly launched innovations.    

The group declared a first interim dividend of 25 sen per share for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2025 (FY2025), payable on June 17.

In a bourse filing on Thursday, the group said as part of the transition to the new manufacturing facility in Bandar Enstek, Negeri Sembilan, Dutch Lady discontinued the production and distribution of some of its non-core Dutch Lady products in 3Q 2024, which partly offset the growth in the core range compared to the same quarter last year. The group stopped operations at the Petaling Jaya factory in Selangor at the end of 2024.

"While innovating our portfolio, we remain committed to working closely with our business partners to deliver high-quality, sustainable, and halal dairy nutrition in line with our purpose of 'Nourishing Our Planet and People in Every Stage of Life'," it added.

On its outlook, Dutch Lady remains cautiously optimistic due to the strength of its brands, and the increasing need for and recognition of the goodness and nutritional value of milk among Malaysians.

"The company will continue to support local dairy farmers, aiming to enhance both the quantity and quality of locally produced fresh milk," it said.

However, it expects the operating environment in Malaysia to remain challenging, shaped by persistent global and domestic uncertainties.

"These include evolving geopolitical developments, continued foreign exchange volatility, rising commodity and dairy raw material (DRM) prices, and regulatory updates that may impact cost structures and supply chains. The ringgit has experienced both sharp appreciation and ongoing instability, compounding uncertainties on import costs. Combined, these factors are expected to drive higher input costs and margin pressures throughout the year," it said.

Dutch Lady shares closed up 44 sen or 1.5% at RM30.10 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM1.93 billion. The stock has retreated 2% year to date.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: YTL Corp's 3Q profit down 16%, dragged by weak YTL Power results, offset by Malayan Cement's strong showing
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): YTL Corp Bhd (KL:YTL) on Thursday posted a 15.5% year-on-year drop in quarterly net profit, dragged by YTL Power International Bhd's (KL:YTLPOWR) weaker results, offset by a strong showing from Malayan Cement Bhd (KL:MCEMENT).

Net profit for the third quarter ended March 31, 2025 (3QFY2025) fell to RM419.38 million from RM496.23 million previously, according to the conglomerate’s bourse filing on Thursday.

Revenue edged up 1.5% year-on-year to RM7.32 billion from RM7.21 billion.

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

The lower earnings came as its largest contributor — the utilities segment via YTL Power, in which the group has a 48.45% direct and 6.41% indirect stake — saw both lower revenue and profit contributions due to the power generation division’s lower pool and retail prices, coupled with the strengthening ringgit versus the Singapore dollar.

As for YTL Power, the group logged a 30% decline in net profit to RM489.41 million from RM698.69 million in 3QFY2024, as revenue slipped 5.2% to RM4.89 billion from RM5.16 billion.

The drag in earnings was offset by a strong showing by its second-largest contributor, the cement and building materials segment, through its 72.09% indirectly owned Malayan Cement.

This segment saw higher revenue on the consolidation of newly acquired NSL Ltd, as well as higher profit on improved operational efficiencies, lower production costs, and reduced borrowing costs.

Malayan Cement booked an 80.6% surge in net profit to RM182.84 million from RM101.24 million in 3QFY2024, in spite of revenue being flat at RM1.1 billion.

YTL Corp’s hotel operations segment also saw a growth in earnings and revenue, thanks to higher occupancy rates and stronger average room rates.

YTL Hospitality REIT (KL:YTLREIT), in which YTL Corp owns a direct 55% and indirect 3.63% stake, saw net property income stay steady at RM79.71 million compared with RM79.79 million for 3QFY2024, as revenue slipped 5% to RM141.1 million from RM148.59 million.

For the first nine months of FY2025, YTL Corp’s net profit dropped 17.1% year-on-year to RM1.33 billion from RM1.61 billion, while cumulative revenue increased 4% to RM23.15 billion from RM22.26 billion.

Looking to the financial year’s final quarter, YTL Corp said the cement and building materials segment is expected to maintain its performance in view of expected stable domestic cement demand.

Meanwhile, the utilities segment’s power generation division will also be expected to see stable demand, it noted, adding that the division will continue to focus on customer service, operational efficiency and exploring diversification beyond the core business into integrated multi-utilities supply.

Shares in YTL Corp ended five sen or 2.45% lower at RM1.99, valuing the group at RM22.11 billion.

YTL Power closed six sen or 1.69% higher at RM3.60, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM29.8 billion. Malayan Cement ended five sen or 1.04% higher at RM4.85, valuing the cement and building materials outfit at RM6.54 billion.

As for YTL Hospitality REIT, its units closed unchanged at RM1.05, valuing the trust at RM1.79 billion.

Edited by S Kanagaraju

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: BYD beats Tesla in Europe for first time with 169% sales surge
data.description:
(May 22): BYD Co sold more electric vehicles in Europe than Tesla Inc for the first time, overtaking the American brand that’s long led the continent’s EV segment.

China’s leading automaker registered 7,231 new battery-electric vehicles in April, according to market researcher Jato Dynamics. That was up 169% from a year earlier, vaulting BYD into the top 10 brands by EV sales. Tesla placed one spot back as its registrations plunged 49%.

“Although the difference between the two car brands’ monthly sales totals may be small, the implications are enormous,” Jato Dynamics analyst Felipe Munoz said in an emailed statement. “This is a watershed moment for Europe’s car market.”

Tesla continued to slump despite accelerating growth in Europe’s EV segment. Registrations rose 28% industrywide, with market leader Volkswagen selling 61% more battery-electric cars in April than a year earlier. Volkswagen AG’s Skoda brand more than tripled its EV registrations.

BYD outsold Tesla by an even wider margin when including the carmaker’s plug-in hybrid electric vehicles. The Chinese company’s total sales soared 359%, Jato said.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Manulife’s 1Q net profit down 76% on lower profit from life insurance business
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Life insurer Manulife Holdings Bhd (KL:MANULFE) saw its net profit fall 76.3% to RM7.13 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) from RM30.1 million a year earlier, on lower profit from its life insurance business.

As a result, earnings per share came in lower at 3.12 sen for 1QFY2025, compared with 13.72 sen for 1QFY2024.

This was despite quarterly revenue rising 5.9% to RM210.78 million from RM198.97 million in 1QFY2024.

The group did not declare any dividend for the quarter under review.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday, Manulife said its life insurance business slipped to a pre-tax loss of RM3.04 million in 1QFY2025 compared with a pre-tax profit of RM3391 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to higher unrealised fair value loss from equity investments.

Looking ahead, Manulife said the industry faces challenges from increasing medical costs, with interim measures to limit premium increases on medical portfolios possibly impacting short-term profitability. However, rising awareness and understanding of medical insurance needs, along with the commitment of all stakeholders in the health ecosystem to drive health reform, will ultimately lead to more affordable and sustainable medical coverage offerings in the market, it added.

On its part, the group will continue to strengthen growth through a robust expansion of its productive and professional agency force, driving growth in bancassurance and cultivating alternative partnerships with a primary focus on new customer acquisition.

As for its asset management business, Manulife has adopted a cautious stance on the local equity market's near-term outlook due to external shocks and uncertainties surrounding international trade dynamics.

"Prolonged uncertainties could elevate downside risks to growth and lead to increased market volatility as trade negotiations unfold.

"However, several factors are poised to support the local equity market in 2025. These include ongoing domestic policy reforms, attractive valuations, appealing dividends, a potential strengthening of the ringgit and a recovery in domestic consumption, all of which are likely to underpin market resilience and performance," it noted.

Manulife shares closed unchanged at RM2.23 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM501 million. The stock has fallen 6.69% so far this year.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: Country Garden debt plan gets nearly 75% of bondholders’ support
data.description:
(May 22): Chinese developer Country Garden Holdings Co has secured backing from nearly 75% of bondholders for its offshore restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of a liquidation hearing next week.

The company needs the support of creditors holding three-quarters of all debt as well as majorities of individual groups of debt holders to pass the plan through a “scheme of arrangement” procedure in Hong Kong. While it is close to securing that level from bondholders alone, so far a key group of bank creditors, known as the co-ordination committee, hasn’t signed on to the plan, the people added.

Country Garden has a winding up hearing scheduled for Monday, as it pursues one of the biggest restructurings by a Chinese developer since the country’s years-long property crisis began. The builder has held lengthy negotiations with offshore bondholders since defaulting on its dollar debt more than 18 months ago. It has US$14.1 billion (RM60 billion) in debt to be restructured, including about US$10.3 billion in outstanding bond principal, according to a January filing.

Country Garden declined to comment.

The builder said in April that it was close to finalising negotiations with the seven banks that make up co-ordination committee, which are long-term business partners of the developer. The committee holds or controls about 48% of three syndicated loans with total principal of US$3.6 billion, according to a company filing.

Country Garden earlier said that it seeks to complete the offshore restructuring in December, according to a company statement.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: BM GreenTech lifts dividend, sees sustained momentum after FY2025 profit rises 56%
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): BM GreenTech Bhd (KL:BMGREEN) expects to continue its growth momentum in the current financial year, after posting a 55.9% year-on-year (y-o-y) rise in its net profit for the financial year ended March 31, 2025 (FY2025) on broad-based improvements across its bio-energy, water treatment and solar energy segments.

The optimism stems from government policies that are supportive towards environmental, social and governance (ESG) adoption, despite rising costs and competition, said the group, which provides integrated engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) solutions in three key operating segments. 

“The group is strategically positioned to enhance its market share by delivering integrated EPCC solutions aligned with sustainability and ESG priorities,” it said while noting challenges from competition, rising costs and geopolitical uncertainties.

Net profit for FY2025 rose to RM52.44 million, from RM33.64 million in FY2024, as revenue increased 27.6% to RM561.64 million from RM440.30 million a year earlier, driven by higher project deliveries and the consolidation of its newly acquired solar energy subsidiary.

Full-year earnings per share improved to 8.91 sen from 6.52 sen in FY2024. The board has proposed a final single-tier dividend of 2.75 sen per share for FY2025 — its first increase in five years, from 1.75 sen per share previously. 

For the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025), net profit grew 33.1% y-o-y to RM15.74 million from RM11.82 million, while revenue climbed 50.0% to RM178.07 million from RM118.70 million.

Quarter-on-quarter, net profit rose 3.8% from RM15.16 million, while revenue advanced 15.4% from RM154.25 million.

Earnings per share for the quarter stood at 2.67 sen compared with 2.29 sen a year earlier and 2.58 sen in the preceding quarter. 

The group’s solar energy segment recorded a more than threefold revenue increase to RM83.48 million in 4QFY2025, driven by new project wins and progress in utility-scale developments. Profit before tax (PBT) for the segment rose 34.0% to RM3.89 million.

The bioenergy segment saw its PBT rise 24.0% y-o-y to RM14.41 million despite a 3.0% dip in revenue to RM75.46 million, supported by improved margins and reversal of impairment provisions.

Water treatment posted a 32.0% jump in revenue to RM19.14 million and a 55.0% increase in PBT to RM3.21 million, supported by stronger project delivery.

BM GreenTech shares closed two sen or 1.1% lower at RM1.84, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.17 billion. The counter is up 5.75% this year.

data.title: Shares in ICT Zone Asia's ACE Market IPO oversubscribed by 1.89 times
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): ICT Zone Asia Bhd (KL:ICTZONE) announced that its initial public offering (IPO) has been oversubscribed by 1.89 times by public investors, ahead of its ACE Market listing.

The tech financing solutions provider scheduled for transfer listing from the LEAP Market to the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia on June 3, offered 154 million shares in total, comprising 133 million new shares and an offer for sale of 21 million shares.

It has received 2,562 applications for 115.06 million shares worth RM23.01 million from the Malaysian public, compared with the 39.77 million shares offered, according to a statement from Malaysian Issuing House Sdn Bhd on Thursday.

The Bumiputera portion was oversubscribed by 1.1 times, with 1,107 applications for 41.71 million shares received. The public portion saw 1,545 applications for 73.35 million shares, representing an oversubscription rate of 2.69 times.

All private placements of both new and existing shares have been fully subscribed, including placement of 89.09 million new shares to identified Bumiputera investors as well as 21 million existing shares to selected investors.

Another 4.14 million new shares that were made available for application by eligible persons have also been fully taken up.

ICT Zone Asia said notices of allotment will be posted to all successful applicants on May 29.

The company is seeking to raise up to RM26.6 million from the issuance of 133 million new shares at 20 sen each, representing 16.72% of its enlarged share capital. Upon listing, ICT Zone Asia is projected to have a market capitalisation of RM159.09 million.

Proceeds from the offer for sale, involving up to 2.64% of its enlarged share base, meanwhile, will accrue entirely to its selling shareholders, ICT Zone Holding Sdn Bhd, and co-founder and non-executive chairman Datuk Seri Ng Thien Phing, who collectively hold 72.85% in ICT Zone Asia currently. 

Ng is also the founder and executive chairman of Main Market-listed property group SkyWorld Development Bhd (KL:SKYWLD). 

Malacca Securities is the principal adviser, sponsor, joint underwriter and joint placement agent for the IPO, while Kenanga Investment Bank is the joint underwriter and placement agent. SCS Global Advisory (M) Sdn Bhd is the financial adviser for the transfer listing.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Dayang bags third Petronas Carigali contract this year, reports lower 1Q profit
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd (KL:DAYANG) has secured a work order from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd for the provision of a landing craft tank (LCT).

The LCT named Dayang Cempaka will be on hire for 256 days from the commencement date, with an option to extend for up to 60 more days, the Sarawak-based oil and gas services group said in a bourse filing on Thursday.

The total contract value will be determined based on work orders issued by Petronas Carigali during the contract period, Dayang said.

This marks the third set of work orders the group has received from Petronas Carigali this year. On April 9, it secured two work orders for the provision of accommodation workboat vessels. Earlier, on March 24, it received three similar work orders.

1Q earnings shrink

Separately, Dayang reported a 56% drop in net profit for the first quarter, weighed down by high operating costs related to vessel preparations for long-term contracts.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) fell to RM12.3 million or 1.06 sen per share, from RM27.9 million or 2.41 sen per share a year earlier.

Revenue declined 38% year-on-year to RM153.8 million from RM247.1 million, mainly due to a lower vessel utilisation rate of 26% versus 48% in the same quarter last year.

The drop was attributed to delays in contract commencement, reduced third-party vessel chartering, coupled with the absence of contract spillovers from previous years.

Compared to the immediate preceding quarter, net profit fell 27% from RM16.9 million, as revenue plunged 51% from RM316.7 million.

The company did not declare any dividend for the quarter.

As of end-March, Dayang’s outstanding estimated call-out contract value stood at RM5.1 billion.

Looking ahead, the group expects activity to pick up in 2QFY2025 as the monsoon season ends, with increased vessel mobilisation and offshore work.

It expects the momentum of these activities to continue for the rest of the year until the monsoon season returns.

Shares in Dayang closed two sen or 1.2% lower at RM1.70 on Thursday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM1.97 billion. Year to date, the counter has fallen over 18%.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Kossan reports 13% rise in 1Q net profit, flags weaker short-term demand
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 22): Kossan Rubber Industries Bhd (KL:KOSSAN) reported a 13% rise in net profit for the first quarter from a year earlier but flagged softer demand in the near term.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) was RM35.7 million or 1.4 sen per share, according to the glove maker’s filing with the stock exchange. Revenue was up 8% year-on-year to RM487.4 million, thanks to higher sales of gloves and clean room products.

The outlook for the coming months, however, is mixed, Kossan said.

In the short term, demand is expected to soften, particularly in the US, Kossan said. Apart from tariff uncertainties, average selling prices in the US are expected to decline slightly due to lower raw material costs and intensified competition, the company noted.

Malaysian glove manufacturers have been facing intensifying market pressure, as Chinese rivals compete aggressively in the global market to counter the sharp decline in orders with higher US import tariffs.

At its technical rubber products division, which makes marine dock fenders and expansion joints in bridges, Kossan said it anticipates moderation in infrastructure project activity this year while demand from the automotive sector would be subdued due to the tariff uncertainties.

Kossan, however, said it is confident of stable performance at its cleanroom division.

By segments, Kossan’s glove division recorded a pre-tax profit of RM36.3 million as revenue rose 9%, supported by higher sales volume and average selling prices.

The cleanroom division saw its pre-tax profit surge 83% to RM4.4 million in 1QFY2025 while revenue grew 12%. However, the technical rubber products division posted a 45% drop in pre-tax profit to RM4.9 million, mainly due to higher operational costs, while revenue fell 7%.

No dividends were announced for the quarter. Kossan last declared an interim dividend together with a special dividend totalling eight sen per share in November.

Shares of Kossan ended down one sen or 0.6% at RM1.65 on Thursday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM4.22 billion ahead of the results announcement. Year to date, the counter has plunged over 41%.

Edited ByJason Ng
